hypothesis: If a curated, standardized dataset of Fe K-edge X-ray absorption spectra paired with local atomic structural representations of iron-containing proteins (XDIP) is constructed through a validated semi-automated pipeline, then machine learning models trained on this dataset will demonstrate measurable predictive performance in both structure-to-spectrum and spectrum-to-property tasks, because the structured pairing of spectral and geometric features provides sufficient information to learn the physical relationships between local iron coordination environments and their corresponding XAS signatures.
